description: Canonical production compatibility & support matrix for the Sentinel Shield engine. Authoritative for scripts/health.sh (fail-closed gate), scripts/doctor.sh (supportability report) and the ci-compatibility.yml matrices. Every range documents its minimum, tested versions, unsupported versions, deprecation policy, a stable expected_failure_reason and the responsible validation suite so an unsupported configuration fails with a stable diagnostic, never an incidental command error.

os.deprecation_policy: An OS family is supported while its vendor ships security updates. Windows is supported only through a POSIX layer (Git Bash, WSL, MSYS2); native cmd/PowerShell is not a supported shell. Removal is announced one minor policy_version ahead.

arch.deprecation_policy: 64-bit x86_64 and arm64 are the only tested architectures. Others are refused rather than silently attempted because the container-backed scanners publish no images for them.

shell.deprecation_policy: All engine scripts are POSIX sh; any POSIX-compliant /bin/sh implementation is supported. Non-POSIX shells (csh/fish/PowerShell) are refused because the scripts are not portable to them.

git.deprecation_policy: Git 2.20 (2018) is the floor: it is the first release with a stable `git -C`, worktree and fetch behaviour the acquisition path relies on. The floor advances no faster than one minor policy_version per year.

jq.deprecation_policy: jq 1.6 is the floor: earlier releases lack `ltrimstr`/`splits`/`@tsv` behaviours the collectors and validators depend on. jq is a HARD dependency of the whole engine.

docker.deprecation_policy: Docker is only needed for container-backed scanners (Semgrep, Trivy, ZAP, Dependency-Check). 20.10 is the floor for BuildKit + pinned-digest pulls. Absent Docker is fine for profiles that do not enable those scanners.

php.deprecation_policy: PHP is required only for PHP-stack profiles (Deptrac/PHPStan/Psalm). The supported band tracks php.net actively-supported branches (8.1-8.4); a branch is dropped ~6 months after it leaves security support.

node.deprecation_policy: Node is required only for Node/React profiles. Only EVEN-numbered Active/Maintenance LTS lines (18, 20, 22) are supported; odd-numbered current lines are refused because they leave support before most release cycles finish.

npm.deprecation_policy: npm majors 8-11 are supported (the versions bundled with Node 18-22 and their upgrades). npm 7 and earlier are refused: their lockfile v2/v3 handling and `npm ci` semantics differ from the immutable-install contract.

pnpm.deprecation_policy: pnpm majors 8-10 are supported. pnpm is provisioned via Corepack; earlier majors used a lockfile format the frozen-lockfile install path does not honour.

yarn.deprecation_policy: Yarn Classic (1.x) and Yarn Modern (2-4) are both supported and resolve to DISTINCT immutable-install commands (--frozen-lockfile vs --immutable). Yarn 5+ is not yet released and is refused until validated.

composer.deprecation_policy: Composer 2.2 (the LTS floor) and later are supported. Composer 1.x is refused: its solver and `--no-plugins` semantics differ from the compat-resolver's assumptions.

case_sensitivity.note: macOS default APFS/HFS+ is case-insensitive. Managed files use case-unique lowercase names, so a case-insensitive filesystem is a warning (degraded), not a hard failure.

assumptions: POSIX file permissions (chmod bits are honoured)
atomic rename() within a single directory (used for temp-then-mv writes)
symlink creation is permitted (transaction containment relies on it)

online_only_operations: docker image pull of pinned scanner digests
composer install / npm|pnpm|yarn install against a package registry

runner_images.deprecation_policy: GitHub-hosted runner images are supported while GitHub itself supports them; an image is dropped from this list within one policy_version of GitHub's own deprecation. Pin to a dated image (ubuntu-24.04) for reproducibility rather than *-latest in release-critical jobs.
